content:
- Ran /bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh as required; it updated current_tasks/current_tasks_done and sent the finished-task email.
- Next closure steps are to tick the remaining task-file publish checkboxes, stage all modified files including .ralph/current_task.txt, commit with explicit gate evidence, and push.

- Updated the task markdown to completed/passes=true with all execution and acceptance checkboxes checked, including the Ralph task switch step.
- No AGENTS.md update is needed from this task; the only notable environment issue was a transient nextest/linker flake that did not block the required top-level gates.
- Next immediate action is git add -A, commit with explicit make check/test/test-long/lint evidence plus the implemented HA coverage summary, then git push and stop.

- Read the Choose-Task progress log, then inspected `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`.
- Found exactly one active candidate task: `.ralph/tasks/story-parallel-ha-test-hardening/04-task-add-non-e2e-api-tls-hostname-and-san-coverage.md` with `<passes>false</passes>` and `<priority>low</priority>`.
- Verified there were no active unblocked bug tasks in `.ralph/current_tasks.md`, and the selected task definition contains no `<blocked_by>` tags.
- Wrote the selected task path to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- Next time, if current task selection changes unexpectedly, re-scan current_tasks plus blockers before touching anything else.

- Read previous codex progress and loaded .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Current task points to .ralph/tasks/story-parallel-ha-test-hardening/04-task-add-non-e2e-api-tls-hostname-and-san-coverage.md.
- Next I am reading the task markdown to determine whether I need to write the first detailed plan with TO BE VERIFIED or continue at a later stage marker.

- Read the task markdown and confirmed it is still not_started with no plan marker present.
- Inspected src/test_harness/tls.rs and src/api/worker.rs enough to see the current gap: fixture generation is localhost-only, while the worker has only a single negative hostname mismatch test.
- Next I am checking existing Ralph task-plan formatting so I can add a detailed plan section plus TO BE VERIFIED in the local style, then stop.

- Searched task-plan examples and the local style is to append a detailed `## Execution Plan` section ending in `NOW EXECUTE` once planning is locked.
- For this task, the concrete gap is clear: `build_adversarial_tls_fixture()` only emits localhost DNS SAN material, while the API worker tests only cover a single `not-localhost` negative case plus CA/expiry and mTLS cases.
- Docs search found no shipped docs that enumerate this non-e2e API TLS hostname/SAN test surface; execution should still audit docs, but likely only needs a no-change confirmation unless a test-coverage page explicitly needs the new scope called out.
- Next I will patch the task markdown with a detailed multi-phase plan that names the fixture/helper changes, the exact positive/negative SAN scenarios, the verification gates, and ends with `TO BE VERIFIED`, then stop.

- Wrote the first-pass detailed execution plan into the current task file and ended it with `TO BE VERIFIED` exactly as requested.
- The plan is specific to the observed code: SAN-aware fixture generation in src/test_harness/tls.rs, targeted API worker TLS helper/test expansion in src/api/worker.rs, optional production-builder parity via src/tls.rs, and a docs audit with direct edits only if a shipped page is actually stale.
- Stopping now at the planning boundary. The next pass must perform a skeptical review, change at least one thing in the plan, and only then replace the marker with `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Loaded previous progress and confirmed the current task path from .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Previous pass already wrote a first-pass detailed plan for the current task and stopped at TO BE VERIFIED.
- Next I am reading the task markdown itself to verify the marker and then I will do the mandatory skeptical plan review, alter at least one thing, and update the plan state accordingly.

- Read the current task markdown and confirmed it was at the TO BE VERIFIED stage.
- Performed a skeptical review against src/test_harness/tls.rs, src/api/worker.rs, and src/tls.rs instead of reusing the first-pass plan blindly.
- Changed the plan in a concrete way: execution must publish named fixture variants via build_adversarial_tls_fixture rather than only exposing a generic SAN builder, and fixture verification must not rely on weak PEM-difference assertions because fresh keys can hide SAN mistakes.
- Also tightened the helper plan so Host-header overrides are explicitly not added unless a concrete ambiguity appears, keeping the focus on rustls server_name validation.
- Replaced the terminal marker with NOW EXECUTE and am stopping here; the next pass should execute the plan as written without further exploratory work.

- Read startup progress log and current task pointer.
- Confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/story-parallel-ha-test-hardening/04-task-add-non-e2e-api-tls-hostname-and-san-coverage.md.
- Previous pass already advanced the task to NOW EXECUTE, so this turn will execute the written plan directly and avoid unnecessary re-exploration.

- Loaded the active task markdown and confirmed it is in NOW EXECUTE state.
- Read the concrete implementation targets in src/test_harness/tls.rs, src/api/worker.rs, and src/tls.rs.
- Verified the current gap: the TLS fixture only emits localhost DNS SAN certs, while API worker tests only cover wrong CA, a single hostname mismatch, expiry, and mTLS.
- Checked doc references and found no shipped page that enumerates this narrow API TLS SAN test matrix, so docs likely stay unchanged unless a concrete stale statement appears.

- Implemented SAN-aware TLS fixture variants in src/test_harness/tls.rs and expanded API worker TLS tests in src/api/worker.rs.
- Added direct fixture SAN inspection using x509-parser and switched the production-builder parity test onto a SAN-rich certificate.
- First make check run failed on a single leftover TestSubjectAltName::Dns literal using &str instead of String; fixing that now, then rerunning the full required gate sequence from the top.
